Jaguar XJ 3.0 SC Petrol Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar XJ 3.0 SC Petrol engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned XJ 3.0 SC engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only. Fitting adds £2,000–£3,500 depending on location and workshop rates. Customers have reported dealer quotes of around £15,000–£30,000 for engine replacement. A low-mileage replacement engine typically ranges from £3,000 to £6,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar XJ 3.0 SC Petrol engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ126 supercharged V6 has a mixed reputation. It's a strong, smooth engine with excellent performance, but it has well-documented weaknesses: the water pump on pre-2015 models is 'poorly designed' and can fail catastrophically, the plastic cooling pipes are a known weak spot, and timing chain tensioners wear with age. Post-2015 models with the revised water pump are significantly more reliable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar XJ 3.0 SC Petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Water pump failure on pre-2015 models — can fail within 20,000 miles and destroy the engine if ignored, (2) Plastic coolant pipe ('V pipe') failure under the supercharger — a well-known weak spot, (3) Timing chain tensioner and guide wear — a design flaw shared with the 5.0 V8, (4) Valve cover gasket leaks and PCV diaphragm failure, and (5) Supercharger coupler wear causing rattling at idle.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar XJ 3.0 SC Petrol engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £8,000–£18,000 XJ, a £4,500–£7,500 recon is 25–90% of the car's value. For a clean, low-mileage example with good body and interior, replacement is often worth it given the XJ's rising classic status. However, if the car has other issues (air suspension, electrical faults, corrosion), evaluate total restoration costs before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar XJ 3.0 SC Petrol engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 3.0 SC uses the AJ126 (also known as 306PS) — a 3.0-litre supercharged V6 producing 340 bhp and 450 Nm. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I replace the water pump preventatively, or does it need to fail first?

acceptedAnswer.text: Preventative replacement is strongly recommended on pre-2015 models. Both the V8 and V6 engines were fitted with a poorly designed water pump until 2015. The obvious solution is to get the pump changed as soon as possible after buying the car. A £400–£800 preventative water pump replacement is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£11,000 engine replacement if it fails and destroys the engine.

eraMap.title: Jaguar XJ 3.0 SC Petrol — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 3.0L supercharged V6 — 340 bhp, 450 Nm. Water pump and plastic coolant pipe failures; timing chain tensioner wear on higher-mileage examples

replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar XJ 3.0 SC Petrol Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an XJ 3.0 SC engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The supercharger and complex cooling system add labour time.

cta.label: → Compare XJ 3.0 SC Petrol engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Water Pump Failure (Pre-2015 Models)
problems.symptoms: Low coolant warning light, coolant loss with no visible leak, overheating, coolant residue around the water pump pulley
problems.typicalMileage: 20,000–60,000 miles — some fail within the first 20,000 miles
problems.repairCost: £400–£800 (water pump replacement; replace the small coolant pipe at the same time)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the low coolant warning appears, pull over within seconds to avoid destroying the engine

problems.recommendation: Both the V8 and V6 engines were fitted with a poorly designed water pump until 2015. Coolant gets inside the bearing housing, corroding the water pump bearing and causing leaks. If you get a "low coolant" alert, stop driving immediately — continuing can destroy the engine. The obvious solution is to get the pump changed as soon as possible after buying the car, ideally to the later revised pump. Replace the small coolant pipe that connects to the pump at the same time.

failureLink.label: Read the full water pump failure guide

problems.title: Plastic Coolant Pipe Failure ("V Pipe" Under Supercharger)
problems.symptoms: Overheating, coolant loss, steam from engine bay, bubbling in the coolant reservoir
problems.repairCost: £600–£1,200 (plastic V pipe replacement with upgraded metal pipes)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the system dumps coolant, pull over immediately and don't restart

problems.recommendation: The plastic "V pipe" under the supercharger is a "well-known weak spot on these engines that often fails catastrophically". When it fails, the system dumps most of its coolant, pressurising and spraying everywhere. This can lead to cracked cylinder heads and a "new engine" scenario. Upgraded metal replacements are available. If you experience overheating or coolant loss, have the plastic cooling pipes inspected and replaced with metal versions as a preventative measure.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant pipe failure guide → /failures/aj126-coolant-pipe-failure/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner & Guide Wear
problems.symptoms: Rattling on cold start, restricted performance warning, engine warning light, metallic noise from the front of the engine
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles — more common on higher-mileage examples

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The AJ126 supercharged 3.0 V6 has the "exact same tensioner/timing chain problems as the earlier AJ133 supercharged 5.0 V8". A design flaw in the tensioners and guides means the steel tensioner piston slowly wears away the aluminium guide button. If the chain jumps, pistons can hit valves. A £1,500–£3,500 preventative chain replacement is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£11,000 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide → /failures/aj126-timing-chain/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Valve Cover Gasket & PCV Diaphragm Failures
problems.symptoms: Burning oil smell, visible oil leaks, oil consumption, lean running error codes
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if you notice oil spots or burning smell

problems.recommendation: The 3.0 V6 gas engine has "coolant issues relating to the pipework and leaking valve cover gasket". Rubber PCV diaphragms rip with age, causing oil consumption and possible lean running error codes. Replacing PCV diaphragms is "a pain" with the supercharger on, so best done when the supercharger is off. If you notice a burning smell or oil spots, have the gaskets inspected promptly.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide → /failures/valve-cover-gasket-leak/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: XJ 3.0 SC Petrol — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Essential preventative work — replace with revised pump
Upgrade to metal pipes for permanent fix
Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Interference engine — pistons hitting valves means full replacement
Catch early to prevent oil contamination

buyingChecks.title: Buying This XJ 3.0 SC Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2015 model with revised water pump — this is the critical item
* Documented cooling system overhaul (water pump, V-pipe with metal replacements)
* Full service history with regular oil changes
* No rattling on cold start — timing chain health is critical
avoidIf: * Pre-2015 model with no water pump history — this is a ticking time bomb
* Any coolant loss or overheating history
* Rattling or ticking from the front of the engine — tensioner warning
* Poor service history — the AJ126 is a complex engine that needs proper maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The XJ 3.0 SC's AJ126 supercharged V6 is a genuinely impressive engine — 340 bhp and 450 Nm in a lightweight, elegant luxury saloon. It's effectively a 5.0-litre V8 with two cylinders removed, sharing much of its architecture with the larger engine. However, it has well-documented weaknesses: the pre-2015 water pump is notoriously unreliable, the plastic coolant pipes fail catastrophically, and the timing chain tensioners wear over time. Buy a post-2015 model with documented cooling system work, or budget for these repairs immediately. Without history? Proceed with extreme caution — the repair bills can be eye-watering.

cta.label: → Compare XJ 3.0 SC Petrol engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar XJ 3.0 SC Petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Water Pump History — Has it been replaced with the revised post-2015 pump? Pre-2015 pumps fail
* Cooling System — Check for coolant leaks; have the plastic V-pipe been upgraded to metal?
* Cold Start — Listen for rattling from the front of the engine (timing chain tensioner warning)
* Coolant Level — Check for frequent top-ups; low coolant warnings are a critical red flag
* Service History — Including the AJ126 engine and confirmed oil change intervals
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the valve covers and PCV system
* Supercharger Noise — Any rattling at idle? (Coupler wear)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for timing or cooling-related codes

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly.
